title: David Deutsch — Constructor Theory and Quantum Computation (Collected)
subtitle: 5 papers on constructor theory, the fabric of reality, and the quantum theory of computation — the constraint space grounding

summary.one_line: Constructor theory expresses physical laws exclusively as which transformations are possible vs impossible — the formal grounding of the constraint space (S, C, A) and the basis for understanding w...
summary.scope: Covers constructor theory, quantum computation, the fabric of reality, and the Church-Turing principle.

sources.label: David Deutsch — Collected Papers. 5 PDFs in /root/projects/blog/library/science/authors/deutsch/.

body.text: Constructor theory replaces dynamical laws with counterfactual principles: certain tasks are possible, others are impossible. The constructor is a physical system that can perform a task repeatedly. The 'possible/impossible' partition IS the constraint relation C. The set of all constructors IS the attractor set A. This is the most fundamental grounding available for the (S, C, A) framework — Deutsch has formalized the structure of the constraint space at the level of physical law.

gaps: Full extraction of all 5 papers needed
